heroHeadline: NJ Transit CEO Defends $150 Fan Fare; Palestine FA Blocked from FIFA Congress — 11 Days to Vancouver Decision
heroSubtitle: NJ Transit's confirmed $150 round-trip fare from Penn Station to MetLife Stadium — nearly 12x the normal $12.90 rate — sparked widespread backlash on April 19, with NJ Transit CEO Kris Kolluri defending the cost and FIFA's COO acknowledging a 'chilling effect' on attendance. Separately, three Palestine Football Association officials including president Jibril Rajoub were denied Canadian visas to attend the April 30 FIFA Congress in Vancouver — where Iran's World Cup participation remains the formal agenda item with 53 days to kickoff.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Los Angeles, Inside World Football, CBC Sports
